# Article Archive

## Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026

URL: https://localblog.trentadvisors.com/blog/medicare-supplement-vs-advantage-in-the-villages-fl-2026/

Skip to main content &#8592; Back to Blog Trent Advisors Contact Trent Advisors &#8594; Home / Blog / Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026 SEO Medicare Supplement vs Advantage in The Villages FL 2026 &#10003; Updated today By Trent Advisors &#183; The Villages, FL &#183; 6 min read &#183; 2026-05-04 &#183; Last verified 2026-05-04 Last reviewed 2026-05-04 by Trent Advisors Quick Facts Call (407) 754-6090 Area The Villages, FL + surrounding cities Pricing $$ — call for quote Table of Contents What Is the Difference Between Medicare Supplement and Medicare Advantage? How Much Is Medicare Insurance in The Villages, Florida in 2026? Which Is Better for Retirees in The Villages? Which Insurance Company Has the Highest Customer Satisfaction in Florida? Common Medicare Mistakes Villages Retirees Make THE VILLAGES &mdash; May 4, 2026 &mdash; Published by Trent Advisors Medicare Supplement vs. Medicare Advantage in The Villages, FL: Which Is Better in 2026? TL;DR: For most retirees in The Villages, FL, Medicare Supplement (Medigap) plans offer broader provider access and lower out-of-pocket costs at a higher monthly premium ($150–$300), while Medicare Advantage plans bundle extras like dental and vision for $0–$80/month but limit you to local networks. The right choice depends on your health, travel habits, and budget — and Trent Advisors (an insurance agency in The Villages, FL) can model both side by side. Medigap Plan G premiums in Sumter County average $150–$300/month in 2026. Medicare Advantage plans in ZIP 32162 often cost $0/month but cap networks. Medigap travels nationwide; Advantage plans usually stay regional. You can switch from Advantage to Medigap, but underwriting may apply after age 65+6 months. The Villages has 80+ Advantage plan options across Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties. In The Villages, FL, retirees who travel often or want to keep specialists in Gainesville, Orlando, or out of state usually save more long-term with a Medicare Supplement plan, while homebound retirees with stable health often prefer Medicare Advantage for the lower monthly cost and bundled extras. Choosing between Medicare Supplement the Villages FL coverage and Medicare Advantage is the single biggest insurance decision most new retirees face here. With more than 145,000 residents — most over age 55 — The Villages (a master-planned retirement community spanning Sumter, Lake, and Marion counties, ZIPs 32162, 32163, 32159) sees this question come up daily at every clubhouse near Lake Sumter Landing and Brownwood Paddock Square. The Villages sits in north-central Florida along US-301 and the Florida Turnpike corridor, with a humid subtropical climate that draws an older-than-average population. U.S. Census data shows Sumter County has the highest median age in the United States at 68.1 years (source: census.gov ). That demographic concentration shapes which Medicare carriers compete here and how aggressively they price. What Is the Difference Between Medicare Supplement and Medicare Advantage? Medicare Supplement is private insurance that pays the gaps Original Medicare leaves behind. Medicare Advantage is an alternative way to get your Medicare benefits through a private plan that replaces Original Medicare. Medigap pays after Medicare; Advantage replaces Medicare with a private network plan. Medigap (a supplemental policy that covers Medicare Parts A and B copays, coinsurance, and deductibles) works alongside Original Medicare. You can see any provider in the country who accepts Medicare. Medicare Advantage (a Part C plan that bundles hospital, medical, and usually drug coverage through a private insurer) is run by carriers like Humana, UnitedHealthcare, and Florida Blue, with HMO or PPO networks. Quick prose comparison Medigap vs. Medicare Advantage: Medigap is the better fit for retirees who travel or want predictable bills, because it pairs with Original Medicare's nationwide acceptance and caps your out-of-pocket exposure. Advantage is the tradeoff plan because it costs less monthly but ties you to a local network and adds prior-authorization steps. How Much Is Medicare Insurance in The Villages, Florida in 2026? Medicare costs in The Villages combine your federal Part B premium plus whatever private plan you choose. Sumter County premiums sit slightly below Florida coastal averages. Expect $185/month for Part B plus $0–$300 for your private plan in 2026. Get help now &mdash; call Trent Advisors &#9742; (407) 754-6090 Industry-average Medicare costs, Sumter/Lake County FL, 2026 Coverage Typical Monthly Cost Out-of-Pocket Max Medicare Part B (federal) $185.00 N/A Medigap Plan G (age 65) $150–$210 $257 deductible Medigap Plan G (age 75) $220–$300 $257 deductible Medicare Advantage HMO $0–$45 $3,500–$8,850 Medicare Advantage PPO $25–$80 $5,500–$11,300 Part D drug plan (standalone) $15–$70 $2,000 cap (2026) Sources: CMS 2026 Plan Finder and Florida OIR rate filings. "Medigap policies help pay some of the health care costs that Original Medicare doesn't cover, like copayments, coinsurance, and deductibles." Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services — medicare.gov Which Is Better for Retirees in The Villages? The better plan depends on three things: how often you travel, which doctors you want to keep, and your monthly cash-flow comfort. Most snowbirds favor Medigap; most year-round residents split evenly. Travelers and snowbirds usually win with Medigap; homebodies on tight budgets often choose Advantage. A typical Villages scenario A common pattern in The Villages: a 67-year-old couple moves down from Ohio, keeps their family doctor up north for summer visits, and golfs five days a week near Spanish Springs. They schedule cardiology check-ins at UF Health Leesburg and dental work in Wildwood. For this couple, a Medigap Plan G plus a standalone Part D plan typically costs more monthly but eliminates network headaches when they head north May through September. A neighbor who never leaves Sumter County and uses The Villages Health primary care often picks a $0-premium Advantage HMO instead because the local network covers everything she needs and adds dental, vision, and hearing benefits at no extra cost. Which Insurance Company Has the Highest Customer Satisfaction in Florida? For Medicare Advantage in Florida, J.D. Power's 2025 study ranked Humana and UnitedHealthcare at the top of member satisfaction. For Medigap nationwide, USAA and State Farm consistently lead. Humana leads Florida Medicare Advantage satisfaction; USAA leads Medigap satisfaction nationally. According to Trent Advisors, the most popular Medicare Advantage carriers in The Villages ZIP codes are Humana, UnitedHealthcare/AARP, Florida Blue, and Aetna. On the Medigap side, Mutual of Omaha, Cigna, and Aetna usually offer the most competitive Plan G rates for Sumter County residents (source: floir.com rate filings). Common Medicare Mistakes Villages Retirees Make The biggest mistakes happen at age 65 enrollment and during the Annual Election Period (Oct 15–Dec 7). Small timing errors create lifetime penalties. Most costly mistakes are missing your Medigap open enrollment window and dropping employer drug coverage too late. Skipping Medigap open enrollment. You have 6 months from Part B start to buy any Medigap plan with no medical underwriting. Miss it, and carriers in Florida can deny you for pre-existing conditions. Late Part D enrollment. A 1% monthly penalty stacks for life if you delay creditable drug coverage. Choosing on premium alone. A $0 Advantage plan with a $8,850 max out-of-pocket can cost more than a $200/month Medigap if you have a hospital stay. Ignoring network changes. The Villages Health and UF Health renegotiate contracts annually; your in-network specialist in 2026 may be out of network in 2027. Myths vs. facts Myth: Medicare Advantage is "free." Fact: You still pay your Part B premium ($185/month in 2026) plus copays and a max out-of-pocket up to $8,850. Myth: You can switch from Advantage to Medigap any time. Fact: After your initial 6-month Medigap window, Florida insurers can medically underwrite and deny you. Myth: All Advantage plans cover the same doctors in The Villages. Fact: Networks differ by carrier; The Villages Health contracts vary by plan year. Myth: Medigap covers prescriptions. Fact: Medigap does not include drug coverage — you need a separate Part D plan. Florida Medicare enrollment data As of 2026, KFF data shows 56% of Florida Medicare beneficiaries are enrolled in Medicare Advantage — well above the 51% national average. Sumter County's penetration runs higher still due to dense carrier marketing in The Villages (source: kff.org ). The Bureau of Trent Advisors Compare Medicare Supplement and Advantage plans in The Villages FL. See costs, coverage, and which fits retirees best.
